subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear sequencing of sections (introduction â data â modelling â evaluation)
Comprehensive table of contents and numbered headings
Wellâorganised conclusion reinforcing the investigationâs aim
weaknesses: Occasional digressions and repetition in variable definitions and rounding justifications
Inconsistent formatting in tables (e.g. dense residuals table)
Reference list lacks a uniform citation style
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates coherent logical development and an unmistakable structure, with only minor digressions preventing it from being âhighly conciseâ. Two of the three strands meet band 3 descriptors and one meets band 4, so an overall score of 3 is appropriate.

strengths: Effective use of multiple representations (tables, scatter plot, box-and-whisker plot, residual table)
Consistent and mostly accurate mathematical notation throughout
Logical explanation accompanying each calculation ensures clarity
weaknesses: Minor typographical slips (e.g. âbecause it because itâ) and a mis-labelled column in Figure 7
Absence of a residual plot limits depth of representation
A few informal notations (mixing Â°C with C, use of âââ) remain
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although a residual plot is missing and there are a handful of minor notational inconsistencies, the student consistently uses correct symbols, links representations clearly to the discussion, and maintains a strong logical thread. This performance aligns with the band 4 descriptors for Mathematical Communication.

strengths: Clear personal motivation rooted in the studentâs own running experience
Primary biometric data collection across diverse environments
Justified modelling choices (e.g. outlier removal) tied to personal training decisions
weaknesses: Mathematical techniques employed are conventional and do not push into highly innovative territory
Creative links (e.g. between uncertainty and training zones) are adequate but not exceptional
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration shows a solid personal approach and significant independent thought, but the methods remain standard for this type of investigation. This matches the band 2 descriptors for Personal Engagement.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on data limitations and model uncertainty throughout
Thoughtful critique of residuals and the impact of uncontrolled variables
Clear evaluation of limitations with practical suggestions for increasing data size and cautioning against extrapolation
weaknesses: Reflection occasionally remains descriptive rather than critically probing statistical assumptions (e.g. linearity)
No quantitative metrics (RÂ², confidence intervals) are provided to support evaluation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides substantial reflection and insightful evaluation of outcomesâgoing beyond basic analysisâbut stops short of fully quantifying model fit. The overall depth and critical nature of the reflections justify a band 3 award.

strengths: Comprehensive application of statistical techniques: summary statistics, outlier testing, Pearsonâs r, least-squares regression, residual analysis and model testing
Correct and thorough calculations of IQR, correlation coefficient and regression parameters (to stated precision)
Demonstrated understanding by applying the model to new data points
weaknesses: Minor formula error in the Pearson correlation denominator notation
Outlier test description omits explicit Qâ and Qâ values
Interpolation could be more directly presented by solving the regression equation for the independent variable
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics used are fully commensurate with SL level and are applied across a broad range of relevant techniques. Despite a few minor procedural oversights, the student demonstrates comprehensive and largely accurate mathematical practice, meriting a band 5 score.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations accompany each calculation and the logical thread is easy to follow.  Occasional typographical slips in the data table and the mis-labelled column in Figure 7 slightly impair clarity, keeping the strand in band 3 instead of band 4.

Calculations of r and regression parameters are correct to stated precision, and interpretation is broadly sound.  Small inconsistencies (incorrect signs in Figure 7 table, rounding in residuals) indicate good but not thorough understanding, placing the work in band 3.

Tables, scatter plot, box-and-whisker plot and a residual table provide varied representations which are clearly linked to the discussion.  However, supporting graphics such as a residual plot or overlay of the regression line are absent, so the usage is âeffectiveâ rather than âhighly effectiveâ.

The investigation is clearly sequenced (introduction â statistics â modelling â evaluation).  Each section follows naturally from the previous one and the argument seldom feels disjointed.  Minor digressions (e.g.
long justifications for rounding choices) interrupt the flow on a few occasions, preventing the piece from reaching the âhighly coherentâ descriptor.

Collecting primary biometric data across five countries and integrating environmental variables shows initiative, but mathematical techniques remain routine.  Creativity is adequate (e.g.
linking uncertainty to training zones) yet not exceptional or innovative.

There is thoughtful analysis of residuals, correlation strength and the impact of uncontrolled variables, but the critique remains fairly descriptive; statistical assumptions (e.g.
linearity) are not probed in depth, so âthoughtfulâ rather than âinsightfulâ applies.

The conclusion recognises limitations, suggests increasing data size and warns against extrapolationâconstituting a clear evaluation with implications.  No quantitative validation (e.g.
using RÂ², confidence intervals) is attempted, so the strand fits band 2.

The student reflects on data limitations, the effect of pace, and model uncertainty throughout the analysis, evidencing meaningful reflection.  Reflection is present in all main sections but not consistently deep or critical enough to reach band 3.

Mathematical symbols (Î£, IQR, r, a, b) are used correctly, and notation is mostly standard.  Minor inconsistencies (mixing Â°C with C, informal âââ) occur but rarely hinder meaning; therefore the work achieves mostly consistent use rather than perfect consistency.

The exploration revolves around the studentâs own running and explicitly connects findings to personal training decisions, demonstrating a clear personal approach.  The methodology itself (linear regression, Pearsonâs r) is conventional, hence band 2 rather than 3.

Although most material directly supports the aim, lengthy narrative about external sources, pace charts and detailed device descriptions could have been summarised.  Repetition when defining variables and justifying rounding suggests the exploration is not yet âhighly conciseâ, yet irrelevant digressions are limited, so the strand meets band 3.

All mathematics employedâoutlier test, summary statistics, Pearsonâs r, least-squares regression, residual analysisâis relevant to the stated aim and applied correctly to primary data, showing effective (but not comprehensive) use.

The student selects personally-gathered data, decides to omit an outlier, and justifies modelling choices, showing significantâthough not outstandingâindependent thought.  Decisions remain standard for this type of task and do not push into highly original territory.

A contents list, numbered headings, and a clear conclusion give the work an unmistakable structure.  Readers can locate material quickly and the progression (questionâmethodâanalysisâreflection) is explicit, matching the exemplar of an introduction-body-conclusion layout.

Statistical techniques are squarely at AA SL level; no concepts beyond syllabus depth are introduced.  Methods match course expectations, giving a âcommensurateâ rather than âfully commensurate and well-appliedâ performance.

annotations.comment: The description of the data collection results is concise and effectively introduces the observed temperature range and sample size.
The raw data table includes numerous columns that are not used in the analysis, which clutters the presentation; consider simplifying to key variables.
The figure caption is verbose; streamline it to describe the key elements of the table and its purpose to improve readability.
There is a duplicated phrase âbecause it because itâ in the scatter-plot justification; correct this typographical error to maintain clarity.
Consider adding a residual plot alongside the scatter plot to assess homoscedasticity and strengthen your multiple representations.
The description of the outlier test mentions IQR but does not show how Qâ and Qâ were derived from the data; include those values for full transparency.
The calculation of IQR is correctly presented and clearly shown, demonstrating understanding of univariate summary measures.
The notation using curly braces for outlier definition is unconventional; interval notation would be clearer for defining y < 152.25 and y > 194.25.
The title is clear and descriptive, immediately conveying the investigationâs focus on modelling heart rate against temperature.
The table of contents is comprehensive and organised with numbered headings and page references that help the reader navigate the exploration.
The introduction effectively grounds the exploration in your personal running experience and clearly motivates the mathematical aim.
Some narrative in the introduction is repetitive (e.g. on variables and justifications); consider condensing to maintain conciseness and focus.
The methodology section describes many uncontrolled variables but lacks a concise outline of the actual mathematical procedure you will use.
Your justification for removing the outlier is descriptive; strengthen it by referencing statistical impact on regression parameters.
Rounding the means to one decimal place more than the raw data is reasonable, but explain why this precision level was chosen.
The correlation grade table is well presented and effectively links râvalue ranges to qualitative descriptions.

The Pearson formula in the denominator is written without a square root applied to both sums, which is incorrect; revise to \(r=\frac{â(x_i-\bar x)(y_i-\bar y)}{\sqrt{â(x_i-\bar x)^2 â â(y_i-\bar y)^2}}\).
Remove the stray LaTeX artifact â\mathcal{V}â in the modelling introduction to maintain a professional presentation.
The general linear equation is stated clearly, but the subsequent explanation repeats points; integrate discussion to avoid redundancy.
You predicted a slope of ~1.58 but calculated ~1.08; discuss factors (e.g. outlier removal, variability) that led to this discrepancy.
Testing the model on a sample point demonstrates application of the regression function; this is good practice for model accuracy assessment.
The residuals table is densely formatted; improve readability by aligning units, shading alternating rows, or summarising key statistics instead.
The discussion of the large residual for (28, 187) insightfully links pace to model error, showing thoughtful analysis of anomalies.
Interpolation was applied by substituting an average temperature instead of inverting y=1.08x+140; solving x=(yâb)/a would be more direct.
Consider mentioning the coefficient of determination (RÂ²) or confidence intervals to quantify model fit and support your evaluation.
The reference entries are inconsistent in format and lack a standard style; adopt a single citation style (e.g. APA) for uniformity.
